evals.name: plane-web-app
evals.prompt: Extract a full MGPC requirements spec from the Plane project management tool. The GitHub repo is at https://github.com/makeplane/plane — read the README and any architecture or feature docs you can find there. I want Mission, Goals, Premises, and Constraints clearly separated.
evals.expected_output: A structured MGPC specification with Mission (single invariant purpose), Goals (measurable outcomes), Premises (assumptions), and Constraints (non-negotiable boundaries) derived from the Plane web app project.

assertions.text: Output contains all four MGPC sections: Mission, Goals, Premises, and Constraints as distinct labeled sections
Mission section contains a single sentence or short statement (not a list), expressing the core invariant purpose
Goals section contains specific, measurable outcomes (feature capabilities, user actions) rather than vague aspirations
Constraints section captures actual hard boundaries (deployment model, license, tech stack, integrations) distinct from goals
Output references specific content from the Plane GitHub repo (specific features, project structure, technology choices) — not just generic PM tool boilerplate
At least one Premise or Constraint is implicit (not stated word-for-word in the source) — e.g. assumptions about users, deployment environment, or integration ecosystem

evals.name: godot-game-engine
evals.prompt: I need a structured requirements spec for the Godot game engine (https://github.com/godotengine/godot). Use the repo README and any architecture or design docs you can find. Extract MGPC: what is Godot's core mission, what goals does it pursue, what assumptions does it operate on, and what are its hard constraints?
evals.expected_output: A structured MGPC specification for Godot Engine: Mission (why it exists), Goals (feature/capability goals), Premises (platform, user, technical assumptions), Constraints (license, performance, compatibility constraints).

assertions.text: Output contains all four MGPC sections: Mission, Goals, Premises, and Constraints as distinct labeled sections
Mission or Premises captures that Godot is free/open-source and community-driven (not just 'a game engine')
Goals section mentions cross-platform export (desktop, mobile, web) as a key capability goal
Constraints section includes the MIT license or equivalent open-source licensing as a hard constraint
The spec reflects game-engine-specific concerns (scripting language, editor, 2D/3D, physics, audio) — not generic software requirements
At least one non-obvious requirement is inferred (e.g. GDScript design goals, no royalties constraint, community governance implications)

evals.name: opentelemetry-architecture-spec
evals.prompt: Extract requirements from the OpenTelemetry architecture specification at https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otel/overview/ — give me a full MGPC spec. What is the system designed to do, what are its goals for implementors, what assumptions does it make, and what are the non-negotiable design constraints?
evals.expected_output: MGPC specification derived from the OpenTelemetry spec: Mission (observability portability), Goals (signal types, SDK/API separation), Premises (language-agnostic, collector model), Constraints (backwards compatibility, vendor neutrality).

assertions.text: Output contains all four MGPC sections: Mission, Goals, Premises, and Constraints as distinct labeled sections
Mission captures vendor-neutral observability portability as the core purpose (not just 'collect telemetry')
Goals mention at least two of the three signal types: traces, metrics, and logs
Constraints or Premises captures the API/SDK separation — that the API must be stable and separately versionable from the SDK
Premises include that the specification is language-agnostic (implemented across many languages)
The spec correctly treats this as a design/architecture document — requirements reflect implementor constraints, not end-user features

evals.name: synthetic-saas-product-brief
evals.prompt:
Extract MGPC requirements from this product brief:

---
FreshRoute is a platform we're building to help independent restaurants cut food waste and delivery costs...
---

Give me a complete MGPC spec.
evals.expected_output: MGPC spec: Mission (cut food waste via last-minute surplus marketplace), Goals (restaurant onboarding <10min, driver add-on workflow, 60-min last-hour feed, 3-city launch), Premises (urban density, Uber Eats/DoorDash ecosystem, small team), Constraints (no card data storage, GDPR, food safety regs, 6-month timeline).

assertions.text: Output contains all four MGPC sections: Mission, Goals, Premises, and Constraints as distinct labeled sections
Mission centers on reducing restaurant food waste / monetizing surplus inventory — not generic 'delivery platform'
Goals section captures specific measurable targets: onboarding <10 minutes, 60-minute ordering window, 3-city launch
Constraints include both GDPR compliance and no-card-data-storage (Stripe delegation) as hard constraints
Premises capture the dependency on existing delivery ecosystems (Uber Eats / DoorDash driver network) as an assumption
Food safety regulatory variance by city is identified — either as a Constraint or an implicit Premise requiring handling

evals.name: stakeholder-interview-transcript
evals.prompt:
Extract MGPC requirements from this stakeholder meeting transcript. Requirements are implicit — you'll need to infer them:

---
[Finance reporting tool replacement meeting transcript]
---

Give me a complete MGPC spec.
evals.expected_output: MGPC spec: Mission (replace legacy Access reporting tool for finance), Goals (15-user concurrent access, <2min monthly report, inline filtering, scheduled reports with email), Premises (SAP + Salesforce as fixed data sources), Constraints ($50k budget, on-prem only, audit trail required, no mobile, Q3 deadline).

assertions.text: Output contains all four MGPC sections: Mission, Goals, Premises, and Constraints as distinct labeled sections
Mission captures the core intent (replace legacy Access reporting tool for finance) — not just a list of features
Goals include both quantified performance targets: 15+ concurrent users AND <2 minute report runtime
Constraints capture both hard constraints: on-prem only AND $50k budget cap
Constraints or Premises capture that SAP and Salesforce integrations cannot be changed (IT constraint)
Audit trail requirement is included with its compliance motivation (not just listed as a feature) — signals regulatory/compliance driver from 'the incident last year'
